steps.title: Extract the Amur grape values from the graph
steps.content: The graph shows leaf pair orientation for three grapevine species. For Amur grape specifically (the dark gray bar), the opposite-side orientation bar reaches approximately 150 pairs, and the same-side orientation bar reaches approximately 60 pairs. These two numbers must appear in the completed example to accurately represent the data.

steps.title: Match the correct numbers to the choices
steps.content: Choice A states “150 leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation, whereas approximately 60 pairs show same-side orientation,” which precisely reflects what the graph displays for Amur grape. Choice B misreads the same-side count as 49 instead of 60. Choices C and D incorrectly claim no same-side pairs exist when the graph clearly shows 60. The 150:60 ratio effectively demonstrates that opposite-side orientation is much more common. That matches choice A — A is correct.
